title: Harnessing analytics by server to increase interactivity of visitor with customer's application
abstract: The current subject matter relates to a computing server that can: (a) transmit, to a client computer, data to execute a program that enables a customer authorized to use the client computer to create multimedia assets, (b) receive multimedia assets from the client computer and store them, (c) publish, based on attributes specified by the user or pre-stored attributes, the multimedia assets on an application (e.g., website) executed on computing devices of visitors, (d) analyze the interaction between the visitors and the multimedia assets as well as between the visitors and the attributes of the displayed multimedia assets to generate an analysis, and (e) modify the multimedia assets in the application in real-time based on at least one of the analysis and one or more preferences of the customer. The application with the modified assets can be more engaging and interactive for visitors of the application.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method comprising: transmitting, by a computing server to a client computer, data to execute a computer program that interacts with a customer authorized to use the client computer to create one or more multimedia assets; receiving, by the computing server and from the client computer, the one or more multimedia assets; publishing, by the computing server and based on at least one attribute of the one or more multimedia assets, the one or more multimedia assets on an application executed on one or more computing devices communicatively coupled to the computing server; analyzing, by the computing server, one or more interactions between the one or more computing devices and the one or more published multimedia assets and between the one or more computing devices and the at least one attribute of the one or more published multimedia assets to generate an analysis; and modifying, by the computing server, the one or more published multimedia assets in the application based on at least one of the analysis and one or more preferences of the customer. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more multimedia assets comprise at least one of an image, a video, an action-based icon, and text. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the at least one attribute of the one or more multimedia assets comprises one or more of: an identification of a multimedia asset of the one or more multimedia assets, a type of animation in the multimedia asset, a number of images in the multimedia asset, a page size of the multimedia asset, a font type for the 

 multimedia asset, a font size for the multimedia asset, and a font color for the multimedia asset. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the type of animation includes one or more of: a hover-over effect associated with multimedia asset, a fade-in effect associated with multimedia asset, a transparency change associated with multimedia asset, and an image enlargement associated with multimedia asset. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the application executed on one or more computing devices is communicatively coupled to the computing server via a content delivery network comprising one or more proxy servers. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the analysis comprises one or more of: an image metric associated with a multimedia asset of the one or more multimedia assets, an attribute metric associated with the multimedia asset, and an experience metric associated with the multimedia asset. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the publishing of the multimedia assets on the application comprises: overlaying the multimedia assets on the application. 8. The method of claim 1, further comprising: storing, by the computing server, the multimedia assets received from the client computer. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein the application is a website. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more computing devices are configured to be operated by one or more visitors of the application. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the modifying of the one or more published multimedia assets in the application occurs in real-time. 12. A system comprising: a user interface module to provide a computer program to a client computer; an application programming interface to receive an identification of one or more multimedia assets and one or more customer preferences from the client computer; and a web module to transmit the one or more multimedia assets to a content delivery network for publication in an application implemented on a computing device, the content delivery network configured to be communicatively coupled to the web module and the computing device. 13. The system of claim 12, further comprising: a database to receive the one or more multimedia assets from the web module, the database configured to store the received one or more multimedia assets. 14. The system of claim 12, further comprising: a controller operably coupled to the user interface module, the application programming interface, and the web module. 15. The system of claim 12, wherein the web module is configured to receive the identification of one or more multimedia assets and the one or more customer preferences from the application programming interface. 16. A non-transitory machine-readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by at least one programmable processor, cause the at least one programmable processor to perform operations comprising: transmitting, to a client computer, data to execute a program that enables a customer authorized to use the client computer to create one or more multimedia assets; receiving, from the client computer, the one or more multimedia assets; publishing, based on at least one attribute of the one or more multimedia assets, the one or more multimedia assets on an application executed on one or more computing devices coupled to the computing server; analyzing one or more interactions between the one or more computing devices and the one or more published multimedia assets and between the one or more computing devices and the at least one attribute of the one or more published multimedia assets to generate an analysis; and modifying the one or more published multimedia assets in the application based on the analysis. 17. The non-transitory machine-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the publishing of the multimedia assets on the application comprises: overlaying the multimedia assets on the application. 18. The non-transitory machine-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the operations further comprise: storing, by the computing server, the multimedia assets received from the client computer. 19. The non-transitory machine-readable medium of claim 16, wherein: the application is a website; and the one or more computing devices are configured to be operated by one or more visitors of the website. 20. The non-transitory machine-readable medium of claim 16, wherein the modifying of the one or more published multimedia assets in the application occurs in real time.
